channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Christian Pulisic's handshake refused by referee, sparks outrage among fans: 'Classless'
item.description: Referee Kevin Ortega refused to shake Christian Pulisic's hand after calling a questionable match against Uruguay, resulting in a 1-0 loss and Copa América knockout for U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Rodgers' whereabouts during unexcused Jets minicamp absence revealed: report
item.description: The New York Jets knew Aaron Rodgers was not going to be present at mandatory minicamp last month, and a new report has revealed where he was.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USA coach Gregg Berhalter should be fired after loss to Uruguay, soccer analyst says
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team fell out of Copa América on Sunday night after a loss to Uruguay, begging the question of whether Gregg Berhalter should stay on as head coach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: San Diegans to shred at 2024 Paris Olympics
item.description: Team USA announced the twelve skateboarders set to represent the Red, White, and Blue in Paris this month at the 2024 Olympic Games, and among them are four San Diego skateboarders.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: US eliminated from Copa America with 1-0 loss to Uruguay, increasing pressure to fire Berhalter
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Gregg Berhalter gave a single-word answer after the United States was eliminated from the Copa America with a loss to Uruguay when asked whether he was the right man to lead the Americans into the 2026 World Cup. “Yes,” Berhalter said.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: When does the USWNT open Olympic soccer play?
item.description: The Americans are back for another attempt at an Olympic gold medal in Paris, something they couldn’t achieve at the Tokyo games when they won bronze.
item.dc:creator: Keith Dunlap, KPRC 2

item.title: Report: Kris Dunn agrees to free agent deal with Clippers
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz have reportedly lost their first free agent of the off-season, as point guard Kris Dunn has apparently agreed to a deal with the Los Angeles Clippers. TNT's Chris Haynes was first to report the deal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The Sharks’ free agency signings were big — for multiple reasons
item.description: San Jose Sharks solidify lineup by signing Tyler Toffoli and Alexander Wennberg to multiyear contracts
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Celtics star Jayson Tatum agrees to 5-year, $314 million supermax extension, AP source says
item.description: Jayson Tatum has agreed to a five-year, $314 million contract extension to remain with the Boston Celtics, a person with knowledge of the situation told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Hightower, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utah HC draft picks begin developmental camp
item.description: PARK CITY (ABC4 Sports) - From NHL first round draft pick on Friday, to working out on the ice in Park City Monday, Tij Iginla and 11 other Utah Hockey Club draft picks have arrived. "It's good, it's fun," Iginla said after the the team's first day of developmental camp in Park City Monday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Klay Thompson’s five most memorable moments with the Warriors
item.description: From scoring 37 points in a quarter to Game 6 heroics at Oklahoma City, Klay Thompson had a legendary run with the Golden State Warriors.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Former Raiders coach Jon Gruden loses bid for state high court reconsideration in NFL emails lawsuit
item.description: Former NFL coach Jon Gruden has lost a bid for three Nevada Supreme Court justices to reconsider whether a lawsuit he filed against the NFL after he resigned from the Las Vegas Raiders in 2021 should be heard in courts or in private arbitration.
item.dc:creator: Ken Ritter, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ownership group of NBA champion Boston Celtics is putting team up for sale
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The ownership group that controls the NBA champion Boston Celtics says it intends to sell all its shares of the team. In a statement released Monday, Boston Basketball Partners LLC said it intends to sell the majority of its shares in 2024 or early 2025.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: France soccer player Koundé 'disappointed' at election results with far-right close to taking power
item.description: DUESSELDORF, Germany (AP) — France soccer player Jules Koundé said he was “disappointed to see the direction our country is taking” after results from the first round of legislative elections left the nation’s far-right party closer than ever of getting into power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Warriors sign shooting guard De’Anthony Melton to one-year contract: reports
item.description: (KRON) – The Golden State Warriors have signed De’Anthony Melton to a one-year contract on Monday, according to The Athletic’s Shams Charania. The news came on the same day it was reported Klay Thompson would be leaving the Warriors to join the Dallas Mavericks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Chicho Arango, Justen Glad named MLS All-Stars
item.description: For the first time in nearly 10 years, Real Salt Lake will have two players represent the club among the best in the league in the MLS All-Star game on July 24.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Epic penalties drama for Ronaldo ends with Portugal beating Slovenia in a Euro 2024 shootout
item.description: A game of epic personal drama and tears for Cristiano Ronaldo has ended with his Portugal making the European Championship quarterfinals, beating Slovenia on a penalty shootout after a 0-0 draw.
item.dc:creator: Graham Dunbar, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lightning's Steven Stamkos bids farewell to Tampa, joins Predators on 4-year, $32M deal
item.description: Lightning's franchise star player, former No. 1 overall draft pick and captain Steven Stamkos bid farewell to the Bolts just as the NHL free agency began on Monday despite the team's general manager saying his resigning was a "top priority.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah Hockey Club will host Chicago in opening game, Oct. 8
item.description: Save the date, Utah hockey fans! The Utah Hockey Club will play its first home game in the NHL regular season on Oct. 8 against the Chicago Blackhawks at the Delta Center.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Sources: Bulls agree with free-agent C Jalen Smith on 3-year, $27M deal, adding youth, frontcourt depth
item.description: CHICAGO — The Bulls have continued their offseason youth movement, signing 24-year-old free-agent center Jalen Smith, formerly of the Indiana Pacers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Magic Johnson admires how Caitlin Clark and Angel Reese deal with the hype while uplifting the WNBA
item.description: Magic Johnson admires how Caitlin Clark and Angel Reese are handling the glaring spotlight of expectations and living up to the moments with record-setting performances while uplifting a league. Johnson knows firsthand what that's like; he and Larry Bird did it 45 years ago.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Simone Biles headlines a U.S. women's gymnastics team eyeing redemption at the Paris Olympics
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — They all had a reason to come back. Every single one of them. Simone Biles to move past those wrenching two weeks in Japan three years ago, when the gymnastics superstar prioritized her mental health and safety over glory, a decision that inspired some and maddened others.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Logano wins at Nashville for 1st NASCAR Cup Series victory of the year
item.description: Joey Logano won the rain-delayed race at Nashville Superspeedway in a NASCAR Cup Series-record fifth overtime Sunday night, stretching his fuel enough to hold off a group of pursuers at the finish line.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Hawkins, Fox 5

item.title: Rockies take final game against White Sox, Jackalopes tie in final game
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — It has been a week since the Grand Junction Jackalopes won a game. After losing the last five games to the Rocky Mountain Vibes, the Lopes looked to close out the series with a win.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: 7v7 Football: Torrey Pines 14, West Hills 0
item.description: In the "Battle on the Mountain" 7v7 Football Tournament at Ramona High School, Torrey Pines defeated West Hills 14-0.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Klay Thompson enters free agency, expected to leave Warriors: reports
item.description: (KRON) — The Golden State Warriors are likely losing a franchise legend. Klay Thompson has entered free agency and is expected to leave the Warriors, according to multiple reports from The Athletic's Shams Charania and ESPN's Adrian Wojnarowski.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulls out of U.S. Olympic trials with leg injury
item.description: Gymnast Shilese Jones pulled out of the U.S. Olympic trials, one day after injuring her left knee while training on the vault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 11 injured after escalator accelerates at Milwaukee Brewers ballpark
item.description: The escalator's malfunction resulted “in an increased downward speed,” the Brewers said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, NewsNation

item.title: 11 injured after escalator accelerates at Milwaukee Brewers ballpark
item.description: Six people were hospitalized and five others were treated at the Milwaukee ballpark after an escalator malfunctioned and had "an increased downward speed.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, PIX 11

item.title: NFL violates antitrust law, Nuggets eye Klay Thompson
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — An eight-person jury ruled in favor of the plaintiffs, ordering the league to pay $14.39 billion in damages. This case centers on the offering of out-of-market Sunday games through a premium subscription service.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: LeBron James to opt out of contract, but plans to return to Los Angeles Lakers: report
item.description: LeBron James will opt out of his player option for next season with the intention of working out a new contract with the Los Angeles Lakers, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Mets' unexpected success in 19-game run boosts wildcard hopes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Why do we watch sports? Distraction, great athletes, team allegiances. Repeat after me: sports are fun. It is great to watch elite-level athletes playing a game at a level that you and I could only dream of. It brings people together, as well as families and friends.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Luke Combs takes the wheel at Ally 400 NASCAR race in Nashville
item.description: Country music superstar Luke Combs will swap his guitar for driving gloves as the honorary pace car driver at the Ally 400 NASCAR Cup Series event on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, NewsNation

item.title: Jury orders NFL to pay nearly $4.8B in 'Sunday Ticket' case
item.description: The lawsuit claimed the league broke antitrust laws by selling its package of Sunday games at an inflated price.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Like father, like son: Lakers select Bronny James in NBA Draft
item.description: The Los Angeles Lakers have selected Bronny James with the 55th overall pick in the 2024 NBA Draft, raising the likelihood that the former USC Trojan will take the court with his father, future Hall-of-Famer LeBron James. Bronny, aka LeBron James Jr.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: FIFA World Cup 26 will generate more than half a billion dollars for Los Angeles: study
item.description: The storylines in store for soccer fans ahead of FIFA World Cup 26 are already sizzling — a potential retirement sendoff for legends like Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, an expanded field of 48 teams and likely the most talented team in United States Men's National Team history — but the tournam
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5

item.title: NBA Draft: Two CU Buffaloes are selected, Nuggets grab big man
item.description: The NBA Draft's first round is in the books as not one but two Colorado Buffaloes heard their names called tonight.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
